summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in the small UAS domain. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry FPVs, and also presented the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. Russian forces have deployed fiber-optic FPV drones in Kharkiv for the first time since the start of the full-scale invasion, with one striking the city and another hitting Perelyub village near the Chernihiv border. In Zaporizhzhia, Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar. Ukraine's I-SEE system is now tracking Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and Motor-G is shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine's largest engine manufacturer. Additionally, Ukrainian BlueBird has updated its Gretz XL electronic warfare system to protect against Mavic drones, and TAF Industries may release a new interceptor on marketplaces this spring.
